title: Phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer
abstract: A phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer having an excitation light source capable of emitting a pulse of light that will induce fluorescence in an external object. A photo detector for detecting the pulse and a circuit to synchronize a pulse of light from the excitation light source with a pulse of light from an external light source.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We Claim: 1) A phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer comprising, an excitation light source capable of inducing fluorescence in an external object; a fluorescence detector for detecting fluorescence in an external object, an external light source capable of generating a pulse of light, and a circuit to synchronize a pulse of light from the excitation light source with a pulse of light from the external light source. 2) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the circuit to synchronize the excitation light source with a pulse generated by the external light source includes an electronic connection between the external light source and the excitation light source selected from the group of a wireless connection and a wired connection. 3) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the circuit to synchronize the excitation light source with a pulse generated by the external light source includes an external reference in communication with the external light source and the excitation light source. 4) A phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer comprising, an excitation light source capable of inducing fluorescence in an external object; a fluorescence detector for detecting fluorescence in an external object, an external light source capable of generating a pulse of light, a photo detector for detecting a pulse of light generated by an external light source, and a circuit to synchronize a pulse of light from the excitation light source with a pulse of light from the external light source. 5) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the excitation light source is selected from the group of a narrow band width emitting semiconductor, a light emitting diode, a laser, a pulsed laser, a pumped laser, a 

 diode-pumped solid-state laser, a flashlamp pumped laser, a pulsed arc-discharge lamp, a xenon flashlamp, a xenon flashlamp in combination with an excitation filter, a continuous-emission lamp, a tungsten halogen lamp, a tungsten halogen lamp with an opto-mechanical interrupter and an excitation filter. 6) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the excitation light source is a laser diode. 7) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the excitation light source is a laser diode between 5mW and 200 mW. 8) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the excitation light source includes a wavelength capable of promoting photosynthesis. 9) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the excitation light source is a laser in communication with a fiber optic cable. 10) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 where in the excitation light source is configured to provide a saturation pulse. 11) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the fluorescence detector is selected from an avalanche photo diode or a photo multiplier tube. 12) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the fluorescence detector is an avalanche photo diode having an active area between 500 脦录脧聙脦鹿 and 800 脦录脧聙脦鹿. 13) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the fluorescence detector is an avalanche photo diode having an optical long pass filter built-in. 

 14) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1 wherein the fluorescence detector is an avalanche photo diode mounted into a collimator tube. 15) A phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer comprising, an excitation light source capable of inducing fluorescence in an external object; a fluorescence detector for detecting fluorescence in an external object, an external light source capable of generating a pulse of light, and a circuit to synchronize a pulse of light from the excitation light source with a pulse of light from the external light source wherein the fluorescence detector is an avalanche photo diode mounted into a collimator tube holding a lens. 16) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 15 wherein the lens is aspherical. 17) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 15 wherein the lens is at an angle to the excitation light source. 18) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1, wherein the fluorescence detector is configured to detect the maximum fluorescence of the external object during saturation of the pulse. 19) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 4 wherein the photo detector for detecting a pulse generated by an external light source is configured to communicate at least one of the frequency, phase and duty-cycle of the pulse of the external light source to a phase synchronizing circuit. 20) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 4 wherein a wired circuit connects the external light source with a phase synchronizing 

 circuit and is configured to communicate at least one of the frequency, phase and duty- cycle of the pulse of the external light source to a phase synchronizing circuit. 21) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 4 wherein a wireless circuit connects the external light source with a phase synchronizing circuit and is configured to communicate at least one of the frequency, phase and duty- cycle of the pulse of the external light source to a phase synchronizing circuit. 22) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 4 wherein the photo detector for detecting a pulse generated by an external light source is a photodiode. 23) A phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer comprising, an excitation light source capable of inducing fluorescence in an external object; a fluorescence detector for detecting fluorescence in an external object, an external light source capable of generating a pulse of light, a photo detector for detecting a pulse of light generated by an external light source, and a circuit to synchronize a pulse of light from the excitation light source with a pulse of light from the external light source wherein the photo detector for detecting a pulse generated by an external light source is in communication with a phase synchronizing circuit. 24) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 23 wherein the photo detector is configured to detect the intensity of the external light source. 25) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 23 wherein the phase synchronizing circuit is configured to determine at least one of the rising edge, the zero crossing, the falling edge, the variable frequency, and the duty-cycle of the pulse generated by an external light source. 

 26) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 23, wherein the phase synchronizing circuit is configured to detect changes in the pulse between 1 Hz and 1 MHz. 27) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 23, wherein the phase synchronizing circuit is configured to detect changes in the pulse between 0 and 100% duty-cycle. 28) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 23, wherein the phase synchronizing circuit is configured to adapt the excitation light to changes in the pulse between 1 Hz and lMHz. 29) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 23, wherein the phase synchronizing circuit is configured to adapt the excitation light to changes in the pulse between 0 and 100% duty-cycle. 30) A phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer comprising, an excitation light source capable of inducing fluorescence in an external object; a fluorescence detector for detecting fluorescence in an external object, a photo detector for detecting a pulse generated by an external light source, and a phase synchronizing circuit to synchronize the excitation light source with a pulse generated by the external light source wherein the phase synchronizing circuit can control at least one of the frequency, phase, intensity and duty cycle of the external light source. 31) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1, wherein at least one of the excitation light and the fluorescence detector are directed toward a single location on the external object. 

 32) The phase synchronizing pulse amplitude modulation fluorometer of claim 1, wherein at least one of the excitation light and the fluorescence detector are directed toward a multiple locations on the external object.
